observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management direct the City Accountant to


a) transfer its remaining balances from the Trust Fund to the General  Fund
   and  reclassify  it  from  Due  to  LGUs  to  Prior  Period   Adjustment
   (Miscellaneous Income);


 b)    coordinate with the City Treasurer to include these prizes as  a  new
   funding source subject to budgetary laws and regulations.

We recommended that the  City  Accountant  provide  for  the  Allowance  for
Impairment to present  fairly  the  receivable  accounts  in  the  financial
statements at net realizable amount  consistent  with  the  requirements  of
IPSAS 29 through the following proposed journal entries:
|                                       |5-05-03-0|147,744.0|         |
|Impairment Loss – Other Receivables    |60       |0        |         |
|Allowance for Impairment – Other       |1-03-06-9|         |47,744.00|
|Receivables                            |91       |         |         |
|Allowance for Impairment – Due from    |1-03-06-0|         |100,000.0|
|NGOs/POs                               |31       |         |0        |
|To recognize allowance for impairment of dormant/non-moving         |
|receivables accounts.                                               |

Moreover, we recommended that  the  City  Accountant  initiate  request  for
authority to write off of the uncollectible accounts from the Commission  on
Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the  Management  direct  the  BAC  to  come  up  with  a
properly consolidated APP based on common programs, activities and  projects
rather than by office, applying  public  bidding  as  the  general  mode  of
procurement except when factors that will allow the use of other  modalities
are present.

We recommended  that  the  Management  submit  sufficient  justification  in
writing as to why the mode  of  procurement  was  changed  from  competitive
bidding despite the absence of a resolution from the  BAC  and  approved  by
the Mayor.


Also, we recommended that the Management  use  competitive  bidding  as  the
general mode of procurement to avoid the impression of  splitting  in  order
to  promote  effective  competition  and  transparency  in  the  procurement
process and obtain an equitable  contract  for  the  best  interest  of  the
government.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the BAC to properly  plan
the procurement of goods and strictly observe the  pertinent  provisions  of
RA  No.  9184  particularly  on  the  use  of  the  alternative   modes   of
procurements  to  promote  transparency,  economy  and  efficiency  in   the
procurement process and in order not to violate the NIRC.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the BAC to  ensure  that  all
procurements  are  in  compliance  with  the  requirements  and   procedures
prescribed in RA No. 9184 and its IRR.

We recommended that the Management review the number  of  organic  personnel
composing the BAC Secretariat so that they will be  able  to  perform  their
functions/  responsibilities  as  required  in  RA  No.   9184.   Also,   we
recommended that the Management assign a BAC Secretariat  member  who  shall
focus on advertising and/or posting the bidding opportunities.  Further,  we
recommended that the Management direct the  BAC  Secretariat  to  coordinate
with the PIO in posting the complete bidding documents, minutes  of  pre-bid
conferences, the NTP and  the  related  approved  contracts  at  the  City’s
website.

We recommended and Management agreed to assign a BAC Secretariat member  who
will post  and  submit  the  APP  and  PMRs  of  the  City.  Henceforth,  we
recommended that the Management  comply  with  the  posting  requirement  to
promote  transparency  and  public  monitoring  of  the  City’s  procurement
process.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Head of the LDRRMO to:

a)    submit the supplemental LDRRMFIP to the LDC for inclusion of the  PAPs
    to the AIP in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No.  2012-
    002 and NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC NO. 2013-1.


b)    ensure strict compliance with the  submission  of  monthly  Report  on
    Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF to the COA  Auditor  to  properly
    monitor the projects and activities and their implementation.

We recommended that management instruct the Inventory Committee to adhere to the provision set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006. Also, management is enjoined to ensure the full implementation of one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to afford management with PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal officials exercise fiscal restraint in the disbursement of funds and require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to limit the disbursement of funds to valid obligations only as appropriated in the Approved Annual Budget to avoid the incurrence of any cash overdraft at year-end. Likewise, strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 337 of RA 7160, otherwise all those found responsible in the incurrence of the cash overdraft will be held personally liable pursuant to Section 158 of GAAM Volume I.

We recommended that management continue making representations with the BIR in order to determine the final amount payable to BIR. After negotiations, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant immediately recognize the amount of liability in the LGU books and provide monitoring of its full settlement.

We recommended that management stop the practice of accommodating reimbursement of expenses and adhere to payments of expenses thru check, except in emergency cases, in keeping with the intents of COA Circular No.97-002 and Section 251 of the Tax Code to ensure withholding of taxes so as help the government in its efficient and effective collection of taxes.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant and the personnel in-charge of GSIS remittances to exert efforts to trace the cause and employees with unremitted premiums. Also, make representation with the GSIS for confirmation of the amount of unremitted contributions to GSIS.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant and the personnel in-charge of HDMF remittances to exert efforts to trace the employees with unremitted premium payment and make representation with the HDMF to confirm the amount of unremitted contributions to HDMF. If unremitted, immediately process payment thereof to avoid penalties.

We recommended that management observe the provisions of COA Circular No.2020-009 on the receipt, recording, distribution, reporting and direct release of donations in-kind and relief goods to the intended beneficiaries. Likewise, we recommended that the MSWDO and MDRRMO submit to the Audit Team the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for the received donations in-kind and actual list of beneficiaries pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No.2020-009. Also, we recommended that management secure documents that would determine the cost of donated items received from the Provincial Social Welfare and Development Office in order to provide basis on the recording of remaining undistributed items, if any.

We recommended that management instruct the MSWDO and MDRMMO to submit the actual list of beneficiaries to determine the nature and quantity of rice distributed and timing of distribution. Also, we recommended that management be mindful of complete submission of supporting documents as attachment to the DVs in keeping with the intents of Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree (P.D.) 1445.

We recommended that management ensure the submission of the list of actual beneficiaries of fertilizers and seeds to support actual distribution thereof to intended beneficiaries. Also, we recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to record items for distribution to its appropriate inventory accounts to provide control over its utilization.

We recommended that management stop the practice of acquiring agricultural supplies on a staggered basis and adhere to the provisions of Section 10, Article IV of R.A.9184 in order to ensure transparency and competitiveness in the procurement process. Likewise, comply with the conditions enumerated in Section 52 and 53, Rule XVI of the Revised IRR of R.A.9184 in the event the original mode of procurement as indicated in the APP cannot be pushed through. Limit the use of alternative mode of procurement only when circumstances warrant and fall within the provisions of Section 52 of the same IRR.

We recommended that management: (a) instruct the Municipal Health Officer to issue certification on the persons who were COVID-19 positive and isolated in the municipal isolation facility so as to provide check and balance; (b) require the personnel in-charge of the municipal isolation facility to certify or provide a list of menu served for the day instead of the IAR; and (c) instruct the personnel in-charge of the municipal isolation facility to have the list of menu for the day signed by the quarantined persons or consider the preparation of a payroll, indicating the cost of meals actually served per day and have it signed by the quarantined persons, to ascertain the regularity, propriety and economy of disbursements.

We recommended that management comply with the documentary requirements for repairs, and maintain records/Subsidiary Ledger of repairs for each property. Furthermore, require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all claims for repairs are supported with complete documentation to ensure that government funds are expended for proper and valid expenses as required under existing regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Agriculture Officer:

trace all the fund sources and programs in the previous years involving the purchase and distribution of breeding stocks so as to fully account the movement of breeding stocks under the custody and monitoring of the Municipal Agriculture Office.

conduct a complete physical inventory of breeding stocks and prepare an inventory report supplying all the necessary data therein, in order to trace the difference between the “should-be” balance per records against the result of the actual count, and have a proper and complete documentation of the inventory of breeding stocks from thereon;

furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of the said report in order to reconcile the report of the Municipal Agriculture Officer with that of the records of the Municipal Accountant and prepare the appropriate documents or certification to support any variance arrived at.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
direct the Heads of Departments in coordination with the Accounting Division to adhere proper compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to protect the interest of the government and to prevent unwarranted disbursement of government funds.
require the Accountant to carefully examine/evaluate Disbursement Vouchers/Payrolls and their supporting documents presented for payment to ensure propriety and regularity of the claim and to avoid audit suspensions.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Terminal Administrator and the City Administrator, with the assistance of the City Engineer, to make a detailed engineering plan that will address the concerns to facilitate a fully operational terminal.

Direct the Terminal Administrator and the Head of Danao City Traffic to mandate all PUV Drivers to park inside the terminal facility while waiting for passengers. By so doing, traffic may be decongested and incidence of parking fee evaders may be avoided. Further, we recommend that the City prepare a traffic scheme to be implemented once the terminal becomes operational.
observations.managementAction: The Sabang sub terminal is already operating and the collection of parking fees is implemented. Building designated as rentable area was already awarded to the winning lessee and management is waiting for the issuance of an SP authority.

observations.recommendation: Review and revisit the Plans and Targets in the operations of the economic enterprise in order to address the areas where the City government can generate additional income. Likewise, we recommend that Management design and/or adopt measures and strategies to remedy the low income from fish port operations and to minimize the expenses in relation to the said enterprise to at least break even or lower than their operating expenses.
observations.managementAction: The plan and program of works for the rehabilitation of the 2nd floor of the port was already done. However, the proposal was for a multipurpose use. The City received the conformation from Philippine Fisheries Development Authority (PFDA).

observations.recommendation: Direct the General Services Officer to make extra efforts to facilitate the titling of lots so that ownership will be transferred in the name of the city.
observations.managementAction: Titling of the land is in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant regularly reconcile the ledgers with cashbook records so as to immediately trace and identify the items which caused discrepancies of Cash in Bank balances between the two independent records to fairly present a reliable amount of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The two offices have already been coordinating each other to reconcile the cash in bank balances and have the common balances at year end.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management assign a specific municipal employee/officer who shall evaluate the current status of the debtor-NGOs/POs to assess the collectability of the outstanding receivables. Furthermore, we recommend that final demand letters be sent to these NGOs/POs requiring immediate liquidation of funds transferred to assess whether or not the purposes therefor were successfully attained.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already sent demand letters the NGOs/POs. However, only the chairman of Danao Agrarian Reform Cooperative (DARC) replied to the demand letter. Other NGOs/POs make representation at the Municipal Accounting Office claiming that they are not the President of the NGOs/POs during the time the funds were transferred to the association and that they are only successors.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the documents required per Item 8.3 of COA Circular No.2016-005 in order to facilitate submission of request for write-off of dormant accounts which are no longer recoverable from deceased LGU official.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office already issued the corresponding journal entry for this.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management ensure timely identification of specific programs/projects for implementation in the ensuing year so as to refrain from including programs/projects described in generic terms in the Annual Budget. Otherwise, management ensure prior authorization of the sanggunian concerned before the incurrence of expenses or entering into contract by the local chief executive so as to prevent disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management has already secured corresponding                         SB resolutions for projects with generic appropriation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to revert the unutilized CY 2018 DRRMF from the GF to TF books, as well as revert the unexpended            CY 2013 DRRMF from the TF to GF books, pursuant to Section 3, Rule 18 of the IRR of RA 10121 and par.5.1.10 of COA Circular No.2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office already issued the corresponding journal entry.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that henceforth, management has to be prudent in the grant of CNA Incentives to its employees by adhering to the provisions and complying with the conditions of pertinent DBM circulars treating thereon, to avoid disallowances in the future.
observations.managementAction: Payment of disallowances was made by responsible employees through payroll deductions until all disallowed amount will become fully paid.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System to coordinate and conduct meeting with the different LGU offices and national government agencies involved in the implementation of the planned GAD programs and activities to formulate strategies in order to ensure the complete implementation of GAD programs and activities in the ensuing year.
observations.managementAction: Management has substantially implemented the GAD PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to verify the cause for the existence of negative balance of the Cash – Local Treasury account and prepare the necessary adjustment in the books, if warranted. Further, we also recommended that corresponding copies of the journal entry voucher and other necessary documents, be submitted to the Commission on Audit, for evaluation. We likewise recommended that henceforth, management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to conduct regular monitoring and analysis of cash accounts especially upon discovery of abnormal balances in order to timely rectify any errors on recording and fairly present the cash accounts in the financial statement in keeping with Section 111(2) of PD 1445 and Section 30, Chapter 3, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to verify the cause for the existence of negative balance of the Cash – Local Treasury account and prepare the necessary adjustment in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit an explanation on the cause of delay of project completion. We also recommended that henceforth, management ensure completion of implemented projects within the time frame set in order to timely deliver the benefits these projects will have provide the public and to avoid accumulation of projects to be implemented and constantly monitored.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted explanation on the delayed implementation of BEA funded projects. Those mention projects were already implemented, has 100% accomplishment both in physical & financial aspects and utilization reports were submitted to COA and PAcco for liquidation purposes.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management initiate efforts in collecting settlement of the afore-cited disallowances from concerned personnel and furnish proof of compliance therewith to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Amount due from any retiring employee was deducted from his receivables from the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the billing statement/statement of account from the GSIS be regularly attached to the disbursement vouchers to facilitate easy review and timely verification of loan deductions made. Likewise, we recommend that management be henceforth, more cautious in computing GSIS deductions in order to avoid refunds to concerned employees for loan overpayments, which consumes man-hours and government resources.
observations.managementAction: Management already ensures attachment of GSIS billing statements to the disbursement vouchers re payment of GSIS premium and loan amortizations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with all the Department Heads in-charge in the supervision and monitoring of a specific program/ project/ activity, cause the prompt and complete submission of fund utilization reports pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No.94-013. Likewise, we recommend that management return the balances of funds transferred to source agencies or request authority from the issuing agency for the utilization of the excess funds. In addition, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare a JEV reclassifying the entries to the account Due to Local Government Units for BEA Funds, instead of Due to National Government Agencies, and properly fill-up the columns in the schedule to attain transparency and consistency in keeping the accounts of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management has already made the necessary adjusting entries. Liquidation reports were also submitted to COA, mostly when already demanded by the source agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management make proper and immediate representation with the concerned source agencies to confirm balances of funds transferred to the LGU and start completing the documentary requirements in order to fully liquidate funds received and return all unused/excess funds pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular 94-013. Should the management consider using the unutilized funds, we recommended that management communicate such request to the source agencies.
observations.managementAction: Management already returned any excess funds from completed projects and intends to continually return whatever excess funds until such time that there will be no unreturned balances and all projects will become fully liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC immediately furnish the Audit Team an authenticated copy of the final and approved Annual Procurement Plan and observe the provisions of Sections 7.1 and 7.2 of the 2016 R-IRR of the Government Procurement Law or R.A.9184 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular No.2012-001 to ensure the efficient discharge of governmental functions and adoption of fiscal discipline measures.
observations.managementAction: Copy of the CY 2020 APP was already submitted by the management to the Audit Team on March 3, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit the list of deposits in transit and other items for adjustment by the bank, indicated in the BRS, and attach the negotiated and cancelled checks thereto pursuant to the pertinent Paragraph 3.0 and 3.5 of COA Circular 96-011 in order to facilitate a comprehensive review on the accuracy of the submitted BRS.
observations.managementAction: Management has attached list of deposit in transit including copies of deposit slips and cancelled checks. A list of negotiated check is also attached to the BRS as part of the bank statement.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management ensure compliance with the provisions of COA Circular 2007-001, to monitor and safeguard government funds, as well as to fairly present the receivable account in the financial statements. Likewise, it is recommended that the Audit Team be furnished copies of any action undertaken by the management, for our review.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already sent demand letters the NGOs/POs. However, only the chairman of Danao Agrarian Reform Cooperative (DARC) replied to the demand letter. Other NGOs/POs make representation at the Municipal Accounting Office claiming that they are not the President of the NGOs/POs during the time the funds were transferred to the association and that they are only successors.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant locate all the supporting documents relative to the receipt of the PDAF money to facilitate its remittance to the National Treasury, pursuant to the Supreme Court en banc decision, so that it can be used with the other priority programs of the government.
observations.managementAction: Management already issued DBP Check No.67528683 dated December 27, 2019 for the return of PDAF to Bureau of the Treasury, which was deposited to the latter’s account on January 31, 2020 per submitted photocopy of deposit slip.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit explanation on the foregoing deficiencies and likewise, submit the Master List of Intended Beneficiaries, for further verification.
observations.managementAction: Management has submitted the required documents to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management submit pertinent documents that would justify the non-inclusion of the Construction of Information Center as one of the projects implemented out of the loan proceeds from DBP. We further recommend that management, henceforth, adequately document all the transactions which transpire throughout the whole loan application process, to facilitate verification and review thereof, and to promote proper accountability and transparency.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to implement the Construction of Information Center because the plans were revised due to inflation and safety reasons. The proceeds from the loan were not enough for the implementation of all the projects applied for. The branch head of DBP issued a certification dated March 1, 2017 on the release of ₱9.779million loan for the said purpose except for the construction of information center.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management immediately submit the lacking copies of the complete set of approved plans/drawings, complete technical specifications, approved PERT/CPM Network Diagram and detailed computation of contract time for the subject projects to facilitate technical review of the contract. It is also recommended that management be more mindful of the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No.2009-001 to avoid suspensions of paid transactions with incomplete documentation.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the required documents to the  Audit Team

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management enforce the submission of the Report on the Utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to COA, monthly and be more mindful of the pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to facilitate timely review of the required LDRMM report.
observations.managementAction: Submission of reports was done by the management and will do its best to submit the same regularly.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit a status report as to the implementation and utilization of the Performance Challenge Fund received from DILG. Likewise, we recommend that management submit an explanation as to the non-submission of bidding documents to COA for evaluation. Moreover, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant draw a journal entry reversing the entry made on December 31, 2014 in order to fairly present the payable and equity accounts in the financial statements. Henceforth, we recommend that management abide by the provisions of COA Circular No.94-103 through the immediate utilization of the funds received, timely and proper liquidation of all implemented projects.
observations.managementAction: The PCF amounting to               ₱1million was already implemented for the project “Concreting of Farm to Market Road Hipotana” with a total project cost of ₱1.06million under contract with YS Construction. Corresponding Liquidation Report was already submitted to the Audit Team. The required adjusting already was also already prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the DAP Manager to send demand letters to debtor-LGU employees requiring settlement of their accounts and gather data as to those individuals who are not connected with the LGU to facilitate sending of demand letters/billing statements. Likewise, we recommend that management instruct the DAP Manager to install more stringent regulations/policies in selling/offering of goods and services on account so as generate more income to the municipality, to prevent further accumulation of receivables and reduce the risk of non-collection thereof.
observations.managementAction: Management intends to make request for write-off for those receivables whose debtors are unknown or cannot be located.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Agriculture Officer to gather data on the whereabouts of the animals comprising the Agricultural Produce for Distribution account and verify existence thereof thru conduct of physical inventory pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I. Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipal Accountant be also furnished with the same report in order to be reconciled with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted documents required by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to trace the difference between the two accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books to arrive at the same account balances in the individual financial statements and eliminate these accounts in the consolidated financial statements. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to make an immediate review and conduct tracing of the differences of the reciprocal accounts once periodic financial statements are generated so as to avoid accumulation of unreconciled differences.
observations.managementAction: These reciprocal accounts were constantly monitored by the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to be prudent in receiving collection of receivables so as to properly recognize payment of accounts in the LGU books and fairly present the amount still for collection. For accounts which are already of remote collection, we recommend that management submit a letter to the COA Commission Proper, thru the Audit Team, requesting for the write-off of such accounts, together with the necessary supporting documents thereto, in order to fairly present the account at its actual realizable amount.
observations.managementAction: A request for write-off was already prepared by the management, for accounts of the deceased debtor. Management exerted efforts to bring down the balances to 162,225.82 as of Dec.31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that henceforth, management be more prudent in the disbursement of funds especially when collection of targeted revenues seem to be unachievable to ensure sound fiscal administration pursuant to the provisions of Section 305 and 337 of R.A.7160.
observations.managementAction: Management has transferred other personnel charged to market operation and assigned them to other departments to lessen the operating expenses of market.  Management has improvised plans to meet the targeted income like tax mapping.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that henceforth, management formulate sound schemes and exercise measures to achieve realizable target revenues and maximize collection thereof in consonance with Section 305 (g), Chapter I, Section 444 (a) and (3) Chapter III of the Republic Act (R.A.) No.7160, relative to municipal budgetary functions.
observations.managementAction: Management tried its best in order to achieve the target income.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management furnish the Audit Team copies of communication or development, if any, on the implementation of these projects. We recommend that henceforth, management ensure completion of implemented projects within the time frame set in order to timely deliver the benefits these projects will have provide the public and to avoid accumulation of projects to be implemented and constantly monitored.
observations.managementAction: Management already submitted the corresponding Certificates of Completion duly signed by the Municipal Mayor and Contractors pertaining to the completion of the six (6) infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the audit team, proofs of rental payments made by the three (3) lessees to determine prompt and complete payment of lease. We further recommend that management expedite the renewal of MOU with the concerned lessees so as to enforce collection of lease rental at a much higher rate which is advantageous to the municipal government. Henceforth, management has to ensure provision of surcharges on the MOUs, as well as actual collection thereof when warranted, so as to preclude delayed payments from lessees.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted documents but are still not complete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer to observe Section 32 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I relative to intact and daily deposit of collections to avoid risk of loss or misappropriation of undeposited collections.
observations.managementAction: Management has tried its best to observe Section 32 of NGAS Manual Volume I, however, this does not seem possible to the management as there are collections which have to be deposited in banks situated in Tagbilaran City, which the management found inconvenient.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that complete set of copies of purchase orders/contract and other pertinent documents required to facilitate auditorial and technical contract review be prepared and submitted COA, prior to project implementation and disbursements pursuant to Items 1.1, 3.1.2 and 3.1.3 of COA Circular 2009-001 and Item 9 of COA Circular No.2012-001. Likewise, it is recommended that the BAC observe Section 23 of the 2016 R-IRR of R.A.9184 in the submission of eligibility documents of bidders, for COA review.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted to the Audit Team, copies of purchase orders with minimal amounts, such as those procured thru shopping, mostly on a quarterly basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management look into the details and reason behind the amount still unremitted to the Philhealth and cause the immediate settlement thereof with Philhealth in order to avoid incurrence of surcharges and/or penalties.
observations.managementAction: Management already started the reconciliations for this unremitted amount thus leaving the balance of Due to Philhealth account of ₱6,274.30 as of Dec. 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prioritize and consider filling–up the vacant positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefit of the constituents.  Otherwise, abolish these items from the Annual Budget and replace them with positions/items necessary for the proper discharge of the Municipality of its functions, targets and programs.  Further, submit justification for the charging of the salaries of the above employees against MOOE funds instead of against PS funds.
observations.managementAction: Management hired personnel to enhance its services and fulfill its mandate.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive conduct a meeting with the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, Municipal Treasurer,  Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant in order to validate the actual existence of the recorded but unidentified CIP – Infrastructure Asset and trace the source agency from which the funds came from. Further, it is recommended that management retrieve all the possible documents that could corroborate the construction of the asset as well as its latest actual physical accomplishment to conform to the proper recording of asset account as stated in the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units, consistent with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).
observations.managementAction: Management started making manual SL for every Construction in Progress accounts to monitor the accomplishment and actual status of the programs/projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management find means to facilitate the creation and maintenance of an active official LGU website to comply with Section 1 of E.O.662, in the spirit of transparency of procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Management still has not created its own official website.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit the necessary documents to prove the eligibility and entitlement of the listed beneficiaries and actual claimants of the social pension. Likewise, it is requested that management submit explanation on the other deficiencies noted, for review.
observations.managementAction: Social Pensions for Senior Citizens are now in the hands of Regional Office. They are now the in-charge of releasing the funds and keeping the records. They already made revalidation process to assess the eligibility of all the beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management correct the project cost indicated in the billboards in order not to mislead the viewing public. Likewise, we recommend that management adhere to the project billboard requirements as specified per COA Circular No.2013-004 to provide complete and accurate information on implemented projects to the public.
observations.managementAction: Management has to revise the plans due to inflation and safety reasons and it was the revised contract cost which was posted in the billboard. Management will now adhere to the posting requirements for project billboard.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require all those concerned employees to retrieve and review all the documents pertaining to NHA Resettlement Project – Phase I to have a clear picture of the project implementation, for transparency sake. Likewise, it is recommended that the management verify the reason as to the non-claim of payment for works/services rendered by the Contractor, in order to fully settle the amount due for liquidation to NHA and clear the books of any idle funds.
observations.managementAction: Management has already negotiated this issue with the NHA. Actual Accomplishment of the project was 100% and the contractor was fully paid by the LGU. But some pertinent supporting documents of disbursement vouchers were nowhere to found. It was misplaced because we were transferred from one place to another due to the earthquake last 2013.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management continue the tracing of documents pertaining to the unsettled payable to GSIS. It is also recommended that management make representation or letter of inquiry to the GSIS, to confirm any unpaid obligation, to expedite the processing of payment.
observations.managementAction: Management continues with the reconciliations of these outstanding obligations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant establish at the beginning of the year, receivables due for collection in the current year, with a corresponding credit to Deferred Real Property Tax Income, based on Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card, as certified by the Municipal Treasurer, pursuant to Section 20 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I. Henceforth, examine the cause of the variance between the beginning balances of Real Property Tax Receivable and Deferred Real Property Tax Income.
observations.managementAction: Management has already started conducting close monitoring of these two accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and Municipal Accountant submit the necessary documents to support the disbursement voucher for cash advance for confidential expenses pursuant to Section 6.1.7, 6.1.8 and 6.1.5 of COA-DBM-DILG-GC for GOCCs-DND Joint Circular No.2015-01, to ascertain the regularity and propriety of the subject transactions.
observations.managementAction: Management still has to submit the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer submit explanation to the noted deficiencies in dates of supporting documents. Likewise, we recommend that the actual certified distribution list of medicines be submitted to COA, for evaluation, pursuant to Sections 4 (6) and (8) of P.D.1445.
observations.managementAction: Management is sorry for the lapses made before and it will implement the recommendation of the audit team in the upcoming purchases.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and web administrator or its equivalent cause the provision for and maintenance of LGU-Danao’s transparency seal on its official website to enhance transparency and accountability on financial transactions and operations thereof pursuant to Section 93 of the General Provisions of GAA of FY 2015.
observations.managementAction: Management has already created its official website but it is not fully functional.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management immediately settle the above suspension through the submission of the foregoing lacking documents as well as justification for the noted deficiencies to avoid disallowance in audit. Moreover, we recommend that management adhere to the pertinent provisions of R.A. 9184 in order to promote transparency, competitiveness and system of accountability in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Management is now gathering data and other lacking documents to settle the said suspension.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that henceforth, management carefully identify only those programs/projects/activities which can be immediately implemented on the scheduled dates in a given year in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No.2017-1, in order to promptly/aptlydeliver the intended benefits due the general public.
observations.managementAction: Management did its best, although not yet enough, thus, ending up in non-implementation of projects under the 20% DF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management provide an explanation on the accumulation of unobligated 20% Development Fund and long overdue implementation of identified projects which is not aligned with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No.2017-1.
observations.managementAction: Some prior year’s appropriations were still not obligated at year-end due to insufficient prioritization of activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit the lacking documents pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No.2012-001, as well as prove the existence of budgetary appropriation for the second partial payment of lots. Likewise, it is also recommended that management revisit the terms of the Deed of Sale and other relevant documents, and make representation with the concerned owners/ relatives/representatives of the owners of the private lots, in order to ensure a smooth-sailing negotiation between the two concerned parties and avoid legal disputes in the future.
observations.managementAction: Management still has to submit the lacking documents pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No.2012-001.

observations.managementAction: Management has still not transferred the ownership of the PCSO-donated ambulance in favor of LGU-Danao.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management henceforth, attach the schedule/ list indicating the purpose for the fund transfer, copy of the latest available bank statement to show insufficiency of funds at FCB-Sagbayan and sufficiency of funds at DBP-Tagbilaran, to ensure proper and complete documentation pursuant to Section 4(6) of P.D.1445.

observations.managementAction: Management still has to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.managementAction: Some prior year’s appropriations were still not obligated at year-end due to insufficient prioritization of activities.

Should the management consider using the unutilized funds, we recommended that management communicate such request to the source agencies.
observations.managementAction: Management still has to make proper and immediate representation with the concerned source agencies as to confirm balances of funds transferred.

observations.managementAction: Management still has to submit the lacking documents pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No.2012-001.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee shall post the  Notice  of
Award  and  Notice  to  Proceed  on  the  procurements  of  goods  and
infrastructure projects through competitive bidding modes of procurement  in
the PhilGEPS website in compliance with the provisions of Section 37 of the
2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184.

We recommended that Management:


   a) include in its consultancy contracts the terms  of  reference  clearly
      defining  among  others  the  Scope  of  Works  including   the   Work
      Program/Schedule and the basis for the derivation  of  the  rates  for
      each hired consultant in order to properly maximize  their  fields  of
      expertise and for proper monitoring of desired outputs.


   b) require all consultants to register  with  PhilGEPS  and  provide  the
      PhilGEPS  registration  number  prior  to  the  procurement  of  their
      services.

We recommended  that  the  Local  Disaster  Risk  Reduction  and  Management
Officer be required to submit the  Report  on  Sources  and  Utilization  of
Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) on or before  the
15th day after the end of each month in compliance  with  COA  Circular  No.
2012-002 for proper evaluation and monitoring.

We recommended that the statutory/mandatory  positions  required  under  the
NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC)  2014-1  dated  4  April
2014 be completely created and filled up to  properly  institutionalize  the
organization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office  and
in order to have  continuity  in  service  and  the  implementation  of  its
programs.

We recommended that Management:


   a) direct the City Social Welfare Officer to submit  a  justification  on
      why such PPAs were funded from the LCPC fund;
   b) ensure that the programs, projects and activities are aligned with the
      DILG MC No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 for the protection of children
      to help reduce, if not eliminate, juvenile delinquency in the City.

We  recommended  that  the  City  fully   establish   and   facilitate   the
accomplishment of its GAD Database containing  sex  disaggregated  data  and
gender statistics to serve as basis for  gender-responsive  plans,  programs
and activities in compliance with the PCW/DILG/ DBM/NEDA Joint Circular  No.
2013-01.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee to properly plan ahead and determine the schedule for physical count per department in order to prioritize and fully realize the conduct of a complete physical count of the supplies inventory and property, plant and equipment of the LGU. Likewise, we recommend that the Inventory Committee prepare the RPCPPE and RPCI and submit copies thereof to COA in order to verify compliance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs. Also, we recommend that henceforth, accounting records be properly and timely reconciled with the results of the physical count in order to effect the necessary adjustments, to fairly present these accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to find time to ensure regular and prompt preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements, as well as submission of the same to the Audit Team pursuant to Item 3.5 of COA Circular No. 96-011 in order for the latter to facilitate a comprehensive review on the accuracy of the reported Cash in Bank balances.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant regularly reconcile the ledgers with cashbook records so as to immediately trace and identify the items which caused discrepancies of Cash in Bank balances between the two independent records to fairly present a reliable amount of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements.

We recommend that management assign a specific municipal employee/officer who shall evaluate the current status of the debtor-NGOs/POs to assess the collectability of the outstanding receivables. Furthermore, we recommend that final demand letters be sent to these NGOs/POs requiring immediate liquidation of funds transferred to assess whether or not the purposes therefor were successfully attained.

We recommend that management instruct the DAP Manager to send demand letters to debtor-LGU employees requiring settlement of their accounts and gather data as to those individuals who are not connected with the LGU to facilitate sending of demand letters/billing statements. Likewise, we recommend that management instruct the DAP Manager to install more stringent regulations/policies in selling/offering of goods and services on account so as generate more income to the municipality, to prevent further accumulation of receivables and reduce the risk of non-collection thereof.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Agriculture Officer to gather data on the whereabouts of the animals comprising the Agricultural Produce for Distribution account and verify existence thereof thru conduct of physical inventory pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I. Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipal Accountant be also furnished with the same report in order to be reconciled with the accounting records.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to trace the difference between the two accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books to arrive at the same account balances in the individual financial statements and eliminate these accounts in the consolidated financial statements. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to make an immediate review and conduct tracing of the differences of the reciprocal accounts once periodic financial statements are generated so as to avoid accumulation of unreconciled differences.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to be prudent in receiving collection of receivables so as to properly recognize payment of accounts in the LGU books and fairly present the amount still for collection. For accounts which are already of remote collection, we recommend that management submit a letter to the COA Commission Proper, thru the Audit Team, requesting for the write-off of such accounts, together with the necessary supporting documents thereto, in order to fairly present the account at its actual realizable amount.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the documents required per Item 8.3 of COA Circular No.2016-005 in order to facilitate submission of request for write-off of dormant accounts which are no longer recoverable from deceased LGU official.

We recommend that henceforth, management carefully identify only those programs/projects/activities which can be immediately implemented on the scheduled dates in a given year in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No.2017-1, in order to promptly/aptlydeliver the intended benefits due the general public.

We recommend that management ensure timely identification of specific programs/projects for implementation in the ensuing year so as to refrain from including programs/projects described in generic terms in the Annual Budget. Otherwise, management ensure prior authorization of the sanggunian concerned before the incurrence of expenses or entering into contract by the local chief executive so as to prevent disallowances in audit.

We recommend that henceforth, management be more prudent in the disbursement of funds especially when collection of targeted revenues seem to be unachievable to ensure sound fiscal administration pursuant to the provisions of Section 305 and 337 of R.A.7160.

We recommend that henceforth, management formulate sound schemes and exercise measures to achieve realizable target revenues and maximize collection thereof in consonance with Section 305 (g), Chapter I, Section 444 (a) and (3) Chapter III of the Republic Act (R.A.) No.7160, relative to municipal budgetary functions.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to revert the unutilized CY 2018 DRRMF from the GF to TF books, as well as revert the unexpended            CY 2013 DRRMF from the TF to GF books, pursuant to Section 3, Rule 18 of the IRR of RA 10121 and par.5.1.10 of COA Circular No.2012-002.

We recommend that management furnish the Audit Team copies of communication or development, if any, on the implementation of these projects. We recommend that henceforth, management ensure completion of implemented projects within the time frame set in order to timely deliver the benefits these projects will have provide the public and to avoid accumulation of projects to be implemented and constantly monitored.

We recommend that henceforth, management has to be prudent in the grant of CNA Incentives to its employees by adhering to the provisions and complying with the conditions of pertinent DBM circulars treating thereon, to avoid disallowances in the future.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the audit team, proofs of rental payments made by the three (3) lessees to determine prompt and complete payment of lease. We further recommend that management expedite the renewal of MOU with the concerned lessees so as to enforce collection of lease rental at a much higher rate which is advantageous to the municipal government. Henceforth, management has to ensure provision of surcharges on the MOUs, as well as actual collection thereof when warranted, so as to preclude delayed payments from lessees.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management expedite the implementation of identified
projects under the 20% Development Fund so that it could be used by the
constituents of the City at the earliest possible time. Further, ensure that the City
Planning Officer prepares the necessary Program of Works to avoid such delays in
the implementation of the projects.

We recommend that the City Mayor required the City Planning and Development
Office and the City Engineer to closely coordinate with each other to facilitate the
implementation of projects.

We recommend that the City Mayor direct the BAC Chairperson to validate the
products listed in the RFQs if it actually met the specifications in the Purchase
Request instead of the practice of waiting for at least three (3) suppliers to quote a
common brand before recommending for the award.

We recommend that Management advise the GSO to expedite the procurement of
goods in compliance with R.A. 9184, so as to facilitate the end-users’ performance of
their functions.

We recommend that the City Mayor:

o. Direct the CENRO, being the de-facto implementer of the Solid Waste
Management, to identify other options to expedite the City’s utilization of the
private Sanitary Landfill located in the Municipality of Consolacion, Cebu.

o. Direct the CENRO, OIC-GSO, and City Engineer to discuss measures to control
the dumping of the wastes in the open dump site. Further, a detailed engineering
plan must be prepared prior to the eventual closure and rehabilitation of the
said dump site.

o. Create a Solid Waste Management Board, by way of an Executive Order, to
institutionalize the City’s implementation of the Solid Waste Management, thus
ensuring proper accountability.

We recommend that the City Mayor direct the CENRO, in coordination with the City Legal Officer, to draft the IRR of City Ordinance No. 38-15 in order to facilitate the proper implementation of the aforementioned ordinance.

We recommend that the City Mayor direct the CENR Officer and the General Services Officer to formulate the schedule of collections for each type of wastes and
inform the stakeholders of the approved schedule. Further, strictly implement a
scheme of “No Segregation, No Collection” to instill discipline among the
constituency and become responsible in disposing their wastes.

We recommend that the City Mayor:
 Direct the Terminal Administrator and the City Administrator, with the
assistance of the City Engineer, to make a detailed engineering plan that will
address the concerns to facilitate a fully operational terminal.
 Direct the Terminal Administrator and the Head of Danao City Traffic to
mandate all PUV Drivers to park inside the terminal facility while waiting for
passengers. By so doing, traffic may be decongested and incidence of parking fee
evaders may be avoided. Further, we recommend that the City prepare a traffic
scheme to be implemented once the terminal becomes operational.

We recommend that Management review and revisit the Plans and Targets in the
operations of the economic enterprise in order to address the areas where the City
government can generate additional income. Likewise, we recommend that
Management design and/or adopt measures and strategies to remedy the low
income from fish port operations and to minimize the expenses in relation to the
said enterprise to at least break-even or lower than their operating expenses.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee
to properly plan ahead and determine the schedule for physical count per department in
order to prioritize and fully realize the conduct of a complete physical count of the
supplies inventory and property, plant and equipment of the LGU. Likewise, we
recommend that the Inventory Committee prepare the RPCPPE and RPCI and submit
copies thereof to COA in order to verify compliance with Section 124 of the NGAS
Manual for LGUs. Also, we recommend that henceforth, accounting records be properly
and timely reconciled with the results of the physical count in order to effect the necessary
adjustments, to fairly present these accounts in the financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive conduct a meeting with the
Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, Municipal
Treasurer, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant in order to validate the
actual existence of the recorded but unidentified CIP – Infrastructure Asset and trace the
source agency from which the funds came from. Further, it is recommended that
management retrieve all the possible documents that could corroborate the construction
of the asset as well as its latest actual physical accomplishment to conform to the proper
recording of asset account as stated in the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local
Government Units, consistent with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards
(PPSAS).

Recommendation:
We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant to verify the cause for the existence of negative balance of the Cash
– Local Treasury account and prepare the necessary adjustment in the books, if
warranted. Further, we also recommended that corresponding copies of the journal entry
voucher and other necessary documents, be submitted to the Commission on Audit, for
evaluation. We likewise recommended that henceforth, management require the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to conduct regular monitoring and
analysis of cash accounts especially upon discovery of abnormal balances in order to
timely rectify any errors on recording and fairly present the cash accounts in the financial
statement in keeping with Section 111(2) of PD 1445 and Section 30, Chapter 3, Volume I
of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace the unreconciled difference
between the two accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books to arrive at
the same balances in the individual financial statements and eliminate these accounts in
the consolidated financial statements. Likewise, we recommended that henceforth,
management make an immediate review and conduct tracing of the differences between
the two accounts once periodic financial statements are generated so as to avoid
accumulation of unreconciled differences.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer to observe
Section 32 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I relative to intact and daily deposit of
collections to avoid risk of loss or misappropriation of undeposited collections.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to verify
the recorded account balances per fund and review the recorded transactions for possible
erroneous recording in order to make the necessary adjustments, if warranted, and
furnish the Audit Team copies of the adjusting entries and supporting documents
therefor. It is recommended that henceforth, management ensure compliance with BIR
Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 on withholding of taxes and timely remittance of the same to
avoid incurrence of penalties/surcharges and fines. Moreover, we recommended that the
Municipal Accountant review the recorded transactions for possible erroneous recording.

Recommendation:
We recommended that complete set of copies of purchase orders/contract and
other pertinent documents required to facilitate auditorial and technical contract review
be prepared and submitted COA, prior to project implementation and disbursements
pursuant to Items 1.1, 3.1.2 and 3.1.3 of COA Circular 2009-001 and Item 9 of COA
Circular No.2012-001. Likewise, it is recommended that the BAC observe Section 23 of the
2016 R-IRR of R.A.9184 in the submission of eligibility documents of bidders, for COA
review.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management submit an explanation on the cause of delay of
project completion. We also recommended that henceforth, management ensure
completion of implemented projects within the time frame set in order to timely deliver
the benefits these projects will have provide the public and to avoid accumulation of
projects to be implemented and constantly monitored.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management provide an explanation on the accumulation of
unobligated 20% Development Fund and long overdue implementation of identified
projects which is not aligned with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum
Circular No.2017-1.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management make proper and immediate representation
with the concerned source agencies to confirm balances of funds transferred to the LGU
and start completing the documentary requirements in order to fully liquidate funds
received and return all unused/excess funds pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular
94-013. Should the management consider using the unutilized funds, we recommended
that management communicate such request to the source agencies.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management provide reasons as to the non-implementation
of identified programs/projects/activities. Likewise, we recommended that henceforth,
management implement programs/ projects/activities on the scheduled dates in a given
year in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular
No.2017-1 in order to deliver the intended benefits due the public or to enjoy the comfort
that the municipal employees of officials could have experienced therefrom.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management require the MDRRMO and Municipal
Accountant to facilitate the immediate transfer of the unexpended balance of the LDRRM
to a special trust fund for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and
management activities within the next five (5) years pursuant to paragraph 5.1.10 of COA
Circular No. 2012-002 and Section 3, Rule 18 of the IRR of RA 10121.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the BAC immediately furnish the Audit Team an
authenticated copy of the final and approved Annual Procurement Plan and observe the
provisions of Sections 7.1 and 7.2 of the 2016 R-IRR of the Government Procurement Law
or R.A.9184 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular No.2012-001 to ensure the efficient
discharge of governmental functions and adoption of fiscal discipline measures.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management find means to facilitate the creation and
maintenance of an active official LGU website to comply with Section 1 of E.O.662, in the
spirit of transparency of procurement process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management create a Disposal Committee to conduct the
immediate disposal of unserviceable properties to prevent further deterioration and
losses; and to allow the City to earn additional income out of the sale of its scrap.

We recommend that the Local School Board be more proactive in identifying and
addressing the needs of the City public schools, facilitate that identified projects are
implemented within the target timeframe, in order to give students and teachers
better facilities which are necessary in providing quality education.

We recommend that Management expedite the implementation of identified
projects under the 20% Development Fund so that constituents of the City will be
benefitted from these projects.

We recommend that the City Engineer prepare the Program of Works and require
the City Planning Development Council to monitor the implementation of the
projects so that intended beneficiaries could maximize the benefits derived
therefrom.

We recommend that the City Treasurer remit the monthly retainer’s fee and
henceforth set aside the ten (10) per centum of the monthly gross income from the
operation of the fish port as retainer’s fee and pay the same on the prescribe due
date to the Philippine Fisheries Development Authority (PFDA) in compliance with the Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommend that The City Mayor , through the City Disaster Risk Reduction
Management Council, hire a permanent CDRRMO who possess the necessary
technical knowledge and skills, education, and/or expertise and Civil Service
Eligibility pursuant to Sections 4.1 and 5.2 of the National Disaster Risk Reduction
Management Council (NDRRMC), Department of Interior and Local Government
(DILG), Department of Budget and Management (DBM), Civil Service Commission
(CSC) Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated 04 April 2014.

Further, we recommend that the Consultancy Services of the Legislative
Department be initiated by the City Vice-Mayor, being the Presiding Officer of the
Sangguniang Panlungsod, notwithstanding the fact that it is still the City Mayor,
being the Local Chief Executive, who will be entering into a contract with the
consultants, as provided by RA 7160. Further, (1) review, acceptance and approval
of the detailed technical contents of the consultants’ reports; (2) supervision; and (3)
recommendation of payment shall likewise rest with the City Vice-Mayor.

We recommend that the City Health Officer prepare a more comprehensive and
detailed program of activities and monitor the implementation of the project to
give the beneficiaries their due for maximum health services that they can avail
therefrom.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory
Committee to conduct a complete physical count of the supplies inventory and
property, plant and equipment of the LGU, prepare the inventory reports and
submit a copy thereof to COA in order to verify compliance with Section 124 of the
NGAS Manual. Likewise, we recommend that the accounting records be properly
and timely reconciled with the results of the physical count in order to effect the
necessary adjustments, thus, fairly presenting these accounts in the financial
statements.

We recommend that management revisit any document in relation to the
donation of ambulance unit by the PCSO and make representation with the PCSO
in order to confirm the actual and rightful owner thereof, to avoid overstating the
assets of the municipality.

We recommend that management submit the lacking documents pursuant to
the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No.2012-001, as well as prove the
existence of budgetary appropriation for the second partial payment of lots.
Likewise, it is also recommended that management revisit the terms of the Deed of
Sale and other relevant documents, and make representation with the concerned
owners/relatives/representatives of the owners of the private lots, in order to ensure
a smooth-sailing negotiation between the two concerned parties and avoid legal
disputes in the future.

We recommend that the management adhere to the guidelines set forth in
DOF Circular No.001-2015 and evaluate the further need of maintaining funds with
FCB instead with government financial institutions. If found justified, we
recommend that management adhere to the guidelines set forth in DOF Circular
No.001-2015 to uphold its purposes, in order to prevent sanctions that may be
imposed by DOF.

We recommend that management henceforth, attach the schedule/ list
indicating the purpose for the fund transfer, copy of the latest available bank
statement to show insufficiency of funds at FCB-Sagbayan and sufficiency of funds
at DBP-Tagbilaran, to ensure proper and complete documentation pursuant to
Section 4(6) of P.D.1445.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit the list of deposits in
transit and other items for adjustment by the bank, indicated in the BRS, and
attach the negotiated and cancelled checks thereto pursuant to the pertinent
Paragraph 3.0 and 3.5 of COA Circular 96-011 in order to facilitate a
comprehensive review on the accuracy of the submitted BRS.

We recommend that management immediately start completing the
documentary requirements in order to fully liquidate funds received and return all
unused/excess funds. Should the management consider using the unutilized funds,
we recommend that management communicate such request to the Source Agencies.
It is likewise recommended that management faithfully adhere to the provisions of
COA Circular 94-013 to avoid incomplete liquidation of fund transfers.

We recommend that management ensure compliance with the provisions of
COA Circular 2007-001, to monitor and safeguard government funds, as well as to
fairly present the receivable account in the financial statements. Likewise, it is
recommended that the Audit Team be furnished copies of any action undertaken by
the management, for our review.

We recommend that management launch an aggressive policy in the grant
and collection of DAP receivables. Furthermore, it is recommended that officials
and employees with huge outstanding balances be no longer allowed to avail of any
DAP services and/or goods, without paying their previous balances. In addition, it is
recommended that management furnish the Audit Team, its accounting system
and/or credit and collection policies, for review.

We recommend that management exert concrete efforts in order to realize
the collectibles from the concerned debtors and furnish the Audit Team copies of
the document showing collection efforts done. Furthermore, for accounts which are
already remote of collection, it is recommended that management submit a letter to
the COA Commission Proper, thru the Audit Team, requesting for the write-off of
such accounts, together with the necessary supporting documents thereto, in order
to fairly present the account at its actual realizable amount.

We recommend that the management initiate efforts in collecting settlement
of the afore-cited disallowances from concerned personnel and furnish proof of
compliance therewith to the Audit Team.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant locate all the supporting
documents relative to the receipt of the PDAF money to facilitate its remittance to
the National Treasury, pursuant to the Supreme Court en banc decision, so that it
can be used with the other priority programs of the government.

We recommend that management submit explanation on the foregoing
deficiencies and likewise, submit the Master List of Intended Beneficiaries, for
further verification.

We recommend that management submit the necessary documents to prove
the eligibility and entitlement of the listed beneficiaries and actual claimants of the
social pension. Likewise, it is requested that management submit explanation on the
other deficiencies noted, for review.

We recommend that management adhere to the pertinent provisions of LBC
No.103 on the proper timing of payment of RATA in order to prudently
management the scarce financial resources of the municipality.

We recommend that management look into the details and reason behind the
amount still unremitted to the Philhealth and cause the immediate settlement
thereof with Philhealth in order to avoid incurrence of surcharges and/or penalties.

We recommend that the management submit pertinent documents that
would justify the non-inclusion of the Construction of Information Center as one of
the projects implemented out of the loan proceeds from DBP. We further
recommend that management, henceforth, adequately document all the transactions
which transpire throughout the whole loan application process, to facilitate
verification and review thereof, and to promote proper accountability and
transparency.

We recommend that management submit explanation for not specifically
identifying the Rehabilitation of Plunge in DAP in any of the issued SB Resolutions
despite previous knowledge of the recommendations of the MGB and volunteer
engineer. Likewise, it is recommended that management provide explanation on the
non-inclusion of the other projects recommended by the volunteer engineer,
contrary to the certification of the MPDC. Lastly, we recommend that the MGB
Report dated February 2014 be submitted, for review purposes.

We recommend that management immediately submit the lacking copies of
the complete set of approved plans/drawings, complete technical specifications,
approved PERT/CPM Network Diagram and detailed computation of contract time
for the subject projects to facilitate technical review of the contract. It is also
recommended that management be more mindful of the pertinent provisions of
COA Circular No.2009-001 to avoid suspensions of paid transactions with
incomplete documentation.

We recommend that management correct the project cost indicated in the
billboards in order not to mislead the viewing public. Likewise, we recommend that
management adhere to the project billboard requirements as specified per COA
Circular No.2013-004 to provide complete and accurate information on
implemented projects to the public.

We recommend that management explain the absence of approval of proper
authority on the foregoing documents. Likewise, it is recommended that
management ensure the proper and timely acknowledgment of receipt of pertinent
documents to fully substantiate the actual occurrence of events during the
procurement and implementation of the projects.

We recommend that the management, especially the Local Development
Council, submit explanation on the bloated income estimate from DAP Operations
and provide bases for doing so. Henceforth, we recommend that management make
a thorough planning and judiciously prepare the Annual Budget in order to project
a more realistic income generated from DAP operations, in aide of prudent fiscal
management.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require all those concerned
employees to retrieve and review all the documents pertaining to NHA Resettlement
Project – Phase I to have a clear picture of the project implementation, for
transparency sake. Likewise, it is recommended that the management verify the
reason as to the non-claim of payment for works/services rendered by the
Contractor, in order to fully settle the amount due for liquidation to NHA and clear
the books of any idle funds.

We recommend that management submit an explanation on the cause of
delay of project completion. We recommend that henceforth, management ensure
completion of implemented projects within the time frame set in order to timely
deliver the benefits these projects shall provide the public and to avoid
accumulation of projects to be implemented and constantly monitored.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the MPDC/OIC-
Municipal Engineer, Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and concerned
department heads to immediately submit copies of the perfected government
contracts, purchase orders already issued/executed, together with the complete
supporting documents, pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No.
2009-001 in order to facilitate timely auditorial and technical review thereof, as well
as ocular inspection of implemented projects. Otherwise, reviewed fund utilization
reports which are deficient in documentation shall be suspended in audit.

We recommend that management provide reasons as to the non-
implementation of identified programs/projects/activities. Likewise, we recommend
that henceforth, management implement programs/projects/activities on the
scheduled dates in a given year in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM
Joint Memorandum Circular No.2011-1 in order to deliver the intended benefits
due the public or to enjoy the comfort that the municipal employees of officials
could have experienced therefrom.

We recommend that management enforce the submission of the Report on
the Utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to
COA, monthly and be more mindful of the pertinent provisions of COA Circular
2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to facilitate timely review of the required
LDRMM report.
We recommend that Management expedite the implementation of identified projects under the 20% Development Fund so that constituents of the City will be benefitted from these projects.
