observations.recommendation: We recommend that management comply with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Circular No.2011-1 on the prompt and full utilization of the 20% Development Fund. Likewise, management identify programs/projects/activities to be implemented and cause implementation thereof on the scheduled dates in accordance with the provisions of the afore-memorandum circular in order to deliver the intended benefits due the public.
observations.reason: Not all projects were implemented in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management advise the Municipal Treasurer to enforce a more vigorous and effective campaign to attain 100% accomplishment in the collection of its targeted income for the year. Likewise, we recommend that the Local Development Council devise and implement a more realistic assessment and estimate of collectible income in order to present a fairer projection of target accomplishment, in terms of collection and implementation of identified programs/projects/activities in a given year, in consonance with the objectives of Section 305 of R.A.7160.

We recommend that management evaluate the causes of the sudden increase in expenses which resulted in the fund’s net loss. Henceforth, we recommend that management be more prudent in the disbursement of funds to ensure sound fiscal administration pursuant to the provisions of Section 305 and 337 of R.A.7160.

We recommend that the Municipal Agriculture Officer (MAO), in coordination with the MPDC, make representation with the DA Field Office 7 as to the original copy of the Sales Invoice and Delivery Receipt issued by FORD TRACTOR Phils., Inc. Likewise, we recommend that the MAO inquire as to the procurement process conducted by DA Field Office 7 in order to obtain a comprehensive understanding as to the processes involved in the implementation of BuB projects. Furthermore, we recommend that the MAO secure copies of the IAR and ARE to determine the actual custodian of the tractor.
observations.reason: The Audit Team will send to COA a copy of the MOA and supporting procurement papers relative to the procurement; DA Field Office 7 updated the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee prohibit processing of requisitions which make reference to specific brand names pursuant to Section 18 of R.A.9184 to ensure acquisition of goods/items at prices most advantageous to the government.

We recommend that management require the LDRRMO to closely monitor the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to ensure that planned program/projects/activities are implemented. We also recommend that management faithfully adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to facilitate timely review of the required LDRMM report.
observations.reason: Still, not all projects were implemented in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and Municipal Accountant submit the necessary documents to support the disbursement voucher for cash advance for confidential expenses pursuant to Section 6.1.7, 6.1.8 and 6.1.5 of COA-DBM-DILG-GC for GOCCs-DND Joint Circular No.2015-01, to ascertain the regularity and propriety of the subject transactions.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive make representation with the LMP-Bohol Chapter to have the proper Official Receipt issued by the latter. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer submit documents or explanation proving the existence of available appropriation for the whole amount of membership dues paid.
observations.reason: No explanation was given by management as to the matter.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer submit explanation to COA on the noted deficiencies in supporting documents. Likewise, we recommend that the actual certified distribution list of supporting medicines be submitted to COA, for evaluation, pursuant to Sections 4 (6) and (8) of P.D.1445.
observations.reason: No documents were submitted for the Audit Team’s evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and web administrator or its equivalent cause the provision for and maintenance of LGU-Danao’s transparency seal on its official website to enhance transparency and accountability on financial transactions and operations thereof pursuant to Section 93 of the General Provisions of GAA of FY 2015.
observations.reason: Still, no transparency seal was maintained by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit an explanation as to the journal entries made debiting the asset accounts and that henceforth, the Municipal Accountant draw the appropriate journal entries to correct the overstatement of PPE accounts and understatement of receivable and payable accounts. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Mayor, as the persons who certified as to the lawfulness and necessity of the transaction as well as the validity, propriety and legality of the supporting documents, submit an explanation as to the absence of any supporting documents attached to the Obligation Request. Furthermore, we recommend that management submit the MOA and other pertinent documents to support the fund transfers.

We recommend that management submit a status report as to the implementation and abide by the provisions of COA Circular No.94-103. Moreover, we recommend that management submit immediately all the procurement documents to COA for proper evaluation.
observations.reason: Liquidation Report submitted to COA in CY 2017 for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit to COA, all pertinent papers relative to the utilization of DILG funds, especially those pertaining to procurement activities, for proper evaluation. Likewise, we recommend that management submit immediately all the procurement documents to COA for evaluation. Furthermore, we recommend that management demand liquidation reports of the funds released and the accomplishment reports of CHDI. Also, require Sanggunian Resolution concurring the Medical Assistance to GCGMH and Infirmary of LGU – San Miguel, otherwise, stop the granting of medical assistance for lack of legal basis.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant provide an explanation as to the journal entry made debiting the expense account Other MOOE instead of the asset account IT Equipment and Software. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant draw the correct journal entry recognizing the asset account. Moreover, we recommend that the Property Officer furnish the Audit Team a copy of the Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment to establish accountability of the actual custodian of the purchased equipment.
observations.reason: Correcting entry already drawn by Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant draw the necessary correcting entries by recognizing the account Due to Other Funds representing the Interest Income earned and eliminating the account Due to Other LGUs in the Trust Fund books. Likewise, we recommend that the receivable account Due from Other Funds and Interest Income be recorded in the General Fund books. At year-end, we recommend that cash equivalent to the total amount of Interest Income earned during the year be transferred from the Trust Fund books to the General Fund books in order to fairly present the year-end financial statements.

We recommend that management submit to COA, all pertinent papers relative to the utilization of NHA funds, especially those pertaining to procurement activities, for proper evaluation. Likewise, we recommend that management submit immediately all the procurement documents to COA for evaluation as well as an explanation as to the non-submission thereof. Henceforth, we recommend that management abide by the provisions of COA Circular No.94-103 through the immediate utilization of the funds received, and proper liquidation of all implemented projects.
observations.managementAction: The Partially Management has not yet implemented
observations.reason: Liquidation Report submitted to COA in CY 2017 for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that henceforth, accomplish the applicable provisions of R.A.9184. We also recommend that the management submit accomplishment reports to support claims for services rendered. Likewise, we recommend that the consultant retain copies of the consultant’s accomplishment reports and that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of accomplishment reports to COA for review.
observations.reason: No submitted consultant work reports were on file; management maintained that consultant was Assistant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prioritize and consider filling–up the vacant positions to enhance implementation of programs, plans and activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefit of the constituents. Otherwise, abolish these items from the Annual Budget and replace them with positions/items necessary for the proper discharge of the Municipality of its functions, targets and programs. Further, submit justification for the charging of the salaries of the above employees against MOOE funds instead of against PS funds.
observations.reason: Some positions were still not filled-up in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that henceforth, management comply with the applicable provisions of R.A.9184 in procuring consultancy services and provide justification as to its non-compliance with the same. Likewise, we recommend that the management require the submission of accomplishment reports to support claims for services rendered.
observations.reason: No full set of consultant documentation submitted to COA for review.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management immediately settle the above suspension through the submission of the foregoing lacking documents as well as justification for the noted deficiencies to avoid disallowance in audit. Moreover, we recommend that management adhere to the pertinent provisions of R.A. 9184 in order to promote transparency, competitiveness and system of accountability in the procurement process.
observations.reason: No settlement has been made by the management as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: a) Require the concerned officials to be vigilant that expenditures should be limited to actual available resources during the year. b) Take up in the books the expenditures and obligations during the year that they are incurred so as to reflect the correct amount in the financial statements. c) In the future, all unpaid expenses at the end of the year should be recorded on Accounts Payable.
observations.reason: Municipal Accountant had been vigilant in charging expenses to current year appropriation.

observations.recommendation: a) Adhere strictly to the prescribed time frame for the submission of financial reports to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 70, NGAS Manual Volume I, COA Circular No. 96-11 and Section 346 of RA 7160. b) Direct the municipal accountant to prepare and submit the year-end financial statements and its supporting schedules on or before March 1 or within 60 days after December 31 of the ensuing year, to COA and other concerned agencies for review and verification.
observations.reason: There were still some reports which were not regularly submitted to COA in CY 2016 due to constraints.

observations.recommendation: a) Record first the release of medical/financial assistance or donation as receivable under account 136-Due from NGAs/ 138- Due from LGUs/ 139-Due from Non-Government Organizations/People’s Organizations as the case may be and require the grantees to submit liquidation documents pursuant to condition No. 7 on the Memorandum of Agreement; b) Require Sanggunian Resolution concurring the Medical Assistance to GCGMH and Infirmary of LGU – San Miguel, otherwise, stop the granting of medical assistance for lack of legal basis; c) Adhere to the provisions cited under Sec. 36 RA 7160 and COA Circular 96-003 for proper implementation and monitoring of funds released for the prevention of irregular, unnecessary and excessive expenditures.
observations.reason: Correcting entry already made; liquidation follow-up ongoing.

observations.recommendation: a) Enforce immediate liquidation of all cash advances and/or refund the excess on unutilized portion of said cash advances, if any, no cash advance shall be granted until full liquidation of the previous cash advance is made. b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor the liquidation of all travel cash advances granted to Officers and Employees of the LGU within 30 days after return from travel. c) Stop the practice of granting additional cash advance for Confidential and Intelligence Fund in the absence of the Credit Advice from COA Central Office or a copy of the duly received transmittal letter by the COA Chairman or her representative and provide copies to the COA Audit Team Leader per COA Circular 2003-003. d) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the cash advances still booked up under the account Due from Officers and Employees (123) to the Account Advances for Officers and Employees (148) pursuant to Accounting Circular Letter No. 2006-001 dated November 9, 2006 for fair presentation of receivable account in the financial statements.
observations.reason: There were still unliquidated cash advances as of year-end although minimal in amount.

observations.recommendation: a) Stop the practice of effecting payment of goods that lacks the required supporting documents in pre-audit as provided in COA Circular No. 2009-002 to avoid disciplinary action against the concerned agency officials and suspensions of transactions in audit that would lead to disallowances. b) Comply and submit the required documents per the previous notice of deficiency. However, the payment for test result has yet to be returned to the municipality since the actual testing was not performed.
observations.reason: There were requirements still pending; Audit Team requested documents; management claimed submission but actual testing was not performed so test results not returned.
